bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.20). I will show a slight willingness to compromise by conceding marginally on my least important issue (issueD) while maintaining my most preferred values for all other issues, aiming for an initial high utility of 0.973.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD'. Because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will continue to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making a minor concession on my lowest-weight issue (issueD) to signal a willingness to move.

The negotiation is still in the very early stages (t=0.06). The opponent has rigidly repeated their opening offer which serves only their utility and provides me with the lowest possible outcome. Since I still have ample time, I will maintain my positions on my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to protect my core interests, while presenting a minor concession on my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to encourage reciprocal movement.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, continually bidding their likely ideal values (valueD for everything). Since we are still early in the negotiation, I will concede fully on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, in hopes of breaking the deadlock and prompting them to reciprocate. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues to maintain a high utility (0.92).

The opponent is holding firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD' without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.10), I need to remain patient and make small concessions to see if I can coax them into movement. I will maintain my top values for my most critical issues (issueA and issueB), while offering modest concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueC and issueD).

The negotiation is still early (t=0.12), so time pressure is very low. However, the opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that yields minimal utility for me, showing no willingness to concede yet. I will maintain my highest utility requests on my most critical issues (issueA and issueB), while offering another minor permutation on issueC and issueD to signal continued willingness to explore combinations.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their initial offer, which gives me my minimum possible utility (0.2). As it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will avoid making large concessions but continue to explore the search space. I will maintain my highest preferred values on the most important issue (issueA) while probing if the opponent prefers a concession on issueB (valueB) instead of issueC or issueD.

The opponent continues to stubbornly offer their most favored position (all valueD), which provides my absolute lowest utility. Since the time fraction is still very early (t=0.16), I am only making small concessions to explore their preferences without giving up much value. I will hold firm on issueA and issueB where my weights are highest, while testing a small concession on issueC and issueD.

The opponent is remaining completely stubborn, offering their absolute best (and my absolute worst) possible values continuously since the beginning of the negotiation. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will continue to make small exploratory concessions on the less important issues and slightly on issueB, probing to see if the opponent is willing to move on any dimension, while keeping my most critical issue (issueA) at its maximum value.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer which provides the lowest possible utility for me, showing zero concession so far. Since the time fraction is still early at t=0.20, I will continue making slight exploratory concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueC and issueD) while holding firm on my top priorities (issueA and issueB), maintaining my utility above 0.86 to prompt them to move.

The time fraction is 0.22, still early in the negotiation. The opponent is stubbornly repeating their opening offer which provides me with my absolute lowest utility. I will maintain high utility for myself by continuing to hold firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), while gently exploring tradeoffs on my lower-weighted issues (e.g., issueC) to see if it encourages the opponent to make a concession.

The opponent continues to repeat their initial offer consistently (giving me my minimum possible utility) without any concessions. As we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain a firm stance on my most important issue (issueA) while offering a new slightly conceded combination on the moderately important issues (issueB and issueC) to see if they prefer this configuration, while preserving a high utility for myself.

The opponent is holding firm on their initial offer of 'valueD' for every issue, which provides me the absolute minimum utility. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueA) while carefully exploring small concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueB and issueD) to find possible value combinations the opponent might accept without compromising my utility too much.

The opponent continues to repeat their opening offer consisting completely of my worst-value issues (`valueD` for all). Since only 28% of the time has elapsed, I will stick to a steady concession rate to elicit some reciprocation without sacrificing too much utility. I will propose an offer that keeps my most valuable issue at its highest utility while slightly compromising on the remaining issues.

The opponent has rigidly held to the exact same offer of `valueD` for all issues since the start, making zero concessions. As we are still at `t=0.3` (early in the negotiation), I will continue to make small concessions, prioritizing preserving my highest value issues (issueA and issueB). This offer explores a concession on issueC while keeping strong utility overall.

The opponent has repeatedly held firm at their opening bid, offering me my worst possible utility across all issues. Given that we are still in the early-to-mid stage (t=0.32), I refuse to make major unilateral concessions. I will instead explore lateral trades by fully conceding on my second-least important issue (issueC) to see if it breaks the deadlock, while holding firm on my highest-priority issues to keep my overall utility above 0.8.

The opponent has not budged from their initial adversarial offer, returning the exact same bid from t=0.00 to t=0.34. Since we are still in the early-mid phase (t=0.34), I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue (issueA) while exploring new combinations on the lesser issues to find a concession they might accept. I will offer a bid targeting a utility around 0.84, maximizing value on issues A and C while compromising on B and D.

The opponent is holding completely firm on their initial offer, making no concessions so far. Since the time fraction is still only 0.36, I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue (issueA) while offering slow, exploratory concessions on lesser issues (issueC, issueD) to find a potential compromise that still gives me a high utility around 0.81. I am waiting for the opponent to show some willingness to negotiate.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening bid of valueD for all issues, offering me minimal utility. At t=0.38, I am still in the early-to-mid stage of the negotiation. To encourage some movement, I will make a moderate concession by lowering my demand on my least important issue (issueD) to match their preference, while maintaining high values on my most important priorities (issueA and issueB). This preserves a strong utility of roughly 0.78 for me.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, offering the same low-utility package (valueD on all issues) since the start. To see if they are responsive to concessions on my highest-priority issue, I will make my first concession on issueA, dropping it to valueB. I will compensate by seeking top values for issueB and issueC to maintain a high overall utility of 0.865 at this stage of the negotiation.

The opponent is holding entirely rigid with their initial offer across all issues, showing no signs of compromise despite reaching nearly halfway to the deadline. To encourage some movement while protecting my most critical priorities, I will maintain my top values for `issueA` and `issueB` (my heaviest weighted issues) and offer further concessions on the lower-priority `issueC` and `issueD`.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (valueD for all issues) across every turn, showing zero willingness to concede so far. As we approach the middle of the negotiation (t=0.44), I will continue to offer gradual, structured concessions on issues of secondary importance (C and D) in an attempt to find a breakthrough. I will maintain issueA at valueA to preserve a high overall utility (0.76) while testing if moderate movement on issues B, C, and D is acceptable to them.

I am approaching the mid-point of the negotiation and the opponent has refused to yield from their initial bid of "valueD" on all issues. Given their hardline stance, I will make a measured concession by offering their preferred value on my least important issue (issueD), and somewhat preferred values on issueC and issueB, while holding firm on my most important issue (issueA).

The opponent remains completely rigid, constantly offering 'valueD' for all issues despite our concessions. As we near the midway point (t=0.48), I will offer a compromise that gives them their preferred 'valueD' on my two least important issues (issueC and issueD) in an attempt to break the deadlock and gauge if they actually value those issues. Since issueA and issueB are my highest priorities (carrying 70% of my total weight), I will hold firm at 'valueA' for these to maintain a high utility of 0.76 while directly appeasing their demands on the other half.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all valueD for every issue. Since we are at the halfway mark (`t=0.50`), I will continue to make concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueC and issueD) down to their preferred valueD to see if it sparks any reciprocity. I will maintain my top value for issueA (my most important issue) and slightly concede on issueB to reach a utility around 0.68.

The opponent is holding entirely rigid, consistently demanding `valueD` for every single issue without offering any concessions. We are just passing the halfway mark (t=0.52). I will maintain my highest-value preference on my most important issue (issueA) while continuing to probe their true preferences by conceding on my less important issues (issueB and issueD) to find a mutually acceptable package, targeting an overall utility around 0.71.

The opponent has rigidly bid `valueD` for all issues since the negotiation started, showing no indication of concession. At `t=0.54`, I need to continue making concessions to probe if they will accept a compromise while preserving a solid utility for myself. I will lower my offer's utility to ~0.68, fully conceding on my least important issue (`issueD`) to the opponent's preferred value, and offering a moderate compromise on the remaining issues.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their initial offer of `valueD` for all issues. As time progresses past the halfway mark to t=0.56, I need to make some significant tradeoffs to figure out which issues they might budge on. I will concede to their preferred `valueD` on my two lowest-weight issues, `issueC` and `issueD`, while maintaining higher values on my critical issues, `issueA` and `issueB`, targeting an overall utility of approximately 0.65.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, continuously asking for their presumed best value 'valueD' across all issues since the beginning. As we pass the midpoint (t=0.58), I need to find compromises that accommodate their demands on my lower-priority issues while protecting my highest-priority ones. I will concede entirely on issueD, partially on issueC and issueB, but hold firm on issueA to maintain a reasonable utility of ~0.65.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all 'valueD' across all issues since the very beginning. At t = 0.6, I am continuing to gradually concede, particularly on issues where I have less utility at stake. I will hold firm on my most important issue (A) but concede my two least valued issues (C and D) completely to their preferred 'valueD', while bumping down my position on B to 'valueC' to continue showing flexibility and probing what they might accept.

The opponent continues to obstinately bid their preferred value on every issue without conceding an inch, despite us being past the midpoint of the negotiation. To try and break the stalemate while preserving my own utility, I will offer a newly structured concession that lowers my utility slightly to ~0.71. I am keeping my top issues (A and B) at high values and conceding on issues C and D where my weights are lowest.

The opponent has rigidly demanded 'valueD' across all issues from the very beginning. To break this deadlock without compromising my most important issue, I will concede fully to their demand on my second most important issue (issueB) and least important issue (issueD), while keeping my highest priority issue (issueA) and third priority (issueC) at my top value. This explores whether they value issueB and issueD highly enough to finally move or accept, while retaining a utilitarian score of 0.68 for myself.

The opponent is holding firm at their initial offer (valueD for all issues), showing no willingness to negotiate thus far. Given the current time fraction of 0.66, I will continue to systematically test lower-utility offers by yielding on my low-weight issues like issueD and partially on issueB, while holding on my highly valued issues or rotating combinations. My target utility for this bid is roughly 0.65 to see if any concessions will elicit a counter-response.

The opponent has rigidly requested "valueD" on all issues in every single offer so far, without conceding. At this stage (t=0.68), it is necessary to present moderate compromises that favor the opponent's requests while preserving enough utility for myself. I will fully concede to "valueD" on my lowest priority issue (issueD), partially concede on issueC, and slightly concede on issueA and issueB, ensuring my utility remains around 0.625.

The opponent is holding entirely rigid at their opening offer consisting of only `valueD` for all issues. Time is advancing (t=0.7), so I will lower my utility target to approximately 0.57. I am fully conceding my two least important issues (issueC and issueD) to `valueD` in order to see if this matches their priorities and induces a compromise, while maintaining `valueB` on my top two priorities.

The opponent continues to stubbornly propose valueD for all issues, making no concessions whatsoever. Since time fraction is at 0.72, I need to find a compromise that might entice them while still securing reasonable utility. I will concede significantly by offering valueD on issues B and D, and valueC on issue C, while holding firm on issueA (my most important issue) at valueA to maintain an overall utility above 0.55.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their extreme opening offer of 'valueD' for every issue, showing zero willingness to concede so far. As we are approaching the late stage (t=0.74), I need to further accommodate their preference for 'valueD' on issues that are less important to me while defending my utility on higher-weight issues. I will offer them 'valueD' on issueB and issueD, aiming for an overall utility around 0.62, giving them their desired values on half the issues.

The opponent has rigidly bid `valueD` for all issues since the start, showing zero willingness to concede or compromise. With the time fraction at 0.76, I need to start making more targeted concessions to find an agreement before the deadline, while still preserving utility on the issues I care most about. I will concede further on the lowest-priority issue while offering moderate compromise on my high-priority issues to test if a middle ground is acceptable without dropping to an unacceptably low utility.

The opponent has stubbornly proposed `valueD` for all issues since the beginning of the negotiation, showing no willingness to concede. With `t=0.78` currently, time pressure dictates a substantial concession but without crossing my reservation curve. I will offer them exactly what they want (`valueD`) on my three least important issues (B, C, and D), while firmly maintaining `valueA` on my most important issue (`issueA`), securing a decent utility of 0.52 for myself.

The opponent has stubbornly repeated their initial offer across all issues (all valueD) without any concession. Given that we are at t=0.8 and facing a potential deadlock, I need to concede further toward their requested values on my less important issues (B, C, and D), while trying to retain at least valueB on my most critical issue (issueA) to maintain a reasonable utility.

The opponent has rigidly held out for `valueD` on all issues until just now, signaling a slight concession on `issueA`. With the deadline approaching (`t=0.82`), I will reciprocate this movement by accepting `valueD` for `issueB` and `issueD` to respect their strong preference, while continuing to seek at least `valueB` on `issueA` (my highest-weight issue) and dropping my ask on `issueC` to `valueC` to propose a balanced compromise.

The opponent has been extremely rigid, bidding all `valueD` almost continuously, except for a brief concession to `valueC` on `issueA` at `t=0.82`. With time running short at `t=0.84`, I will concede fully to their preference of `valueD` on `issueB`, `issueC`, and `issueD` where my weights are lower. In exchange, I will ask for `valueB` on `issueA`, maintaining a utility of ~0.41, testing if they are willing to compromise on the one issue where they showed flexibility.

The opponent has stubbornly demanded valueD on all issues, but has finally shown slight flexibility by offering valueC on issueA. To encourage them to compromise further while protecting a moderate level of my own utility, I will accept their rigid demands for valueD on issueC and issueD, but ask for valueB on issueA and valueC on issueB. This keeps my utility around 0.493 as we approach the deadline.

The time fraction is 0.88, which means the deadline is rapidly approaching. The opponent has rigidly bid 'valueD' for everything for most of the negotiation, but recently signaled a slight willingness to concede on `issueA` to `valueC` during turns t=0.82 and t=0.86. I will offer their exact preferred values on `issueC` and `issueD`, match their concession on `issueA` with `valueC`, and maintain `valueA` on `issueB`, targeting a utility of around 0.55 for myself while matching their observed concessions.

The time fraction is 0.9, meaning the deadline is rapidly approaching. The opponent recently conceded on issueA, shifting their bid from valueD to valueC, while remaining steadfast on valueD for all other issues. To reach an agreement, I will accept their proposal for issueA and maintain my concession to valueD on issues C and D, but ask for valueB on issueB to keep my utility above 0.45.

The opponent has finally conceded on issueA to valueB while holding firm on valueD for issues B, C, and D. Since the deadline is very close at t=0.92, mimicking their most recent offer guarantees that we reach an agreement and avoid the reservation utility of zero. This nets me a utility of approximately 0.412, which is acceptable under this time pressure.
